rationales.self_direction: She says she "stopped chasing the next promotion" and chose a life organized around what matters to her rather than external advancement.
rationales.stimulation: She explicitly says "I'm not looking for excitement from my job" and rejects boredom-avoidance as a goal.
rationales.hedonism: The entry centers on pleasure in "mint tea," sunset light, and the idea that these sensory moments "are the whole point."
rationales.achievement: She frames "the next promotion" and "the next credential" as pursuits she deliberately stopped chasing.

rationales.hedonism: She mourns losing "coffee in the morning," "Thursday evenings at the souk," and "the small things that make it bearable," showing pleasure and peace are what she is trying to protect.
rationales.achievement: She contrasts Tariq's talk of "opportunities for growth" with her own resentment, treating careerist striving as something she does not want.
rationales.security: She says pushing back would make her "visible in the wrong way" and hurt her references, so she chooses safety and stability over confrontation.
rationales.conformity: She says she will "go to the meetings" and remain a "team player" rather than openly resist the new requirements.

rationales.hedonism: She focuses on "good olive oil," warm light, and finding "a new bakery," then concludes that "survival is about noticing what's still good."

rationales.self_direction: She treasures having "nothing required of me" and says "maybe I'll do something else entirely," treating unscheduled choice as freedom.
rationales.hedonism: The session celebrates "lights on a cold street," a warm cookie, long baths, sleep, and rest as the good she wants most.
rationales.achievement: She rejects productivity thinking, saying people "schedule their relaxation" and that real rest begins when you "stop trying to optimize anything."

rationales.hedonism: She says even the "fancy coffee" was "drinking it to finish it, not to enjoy it," showing her day is crowding out pleasure and rest.
rationales.achievement: She recalls choosing "books over activities that might have 'developed my potential,'" and describes the rewarded, ambitious path as alien to her.
rationales.benevolence: She hears "behavioral issues" as code for a student "nobody took time to understand," showing active concern for a specific student's wellbeing.

rationales.self_direction: She deliberately takes "five minutes that I technically don't have" and decides to "make room" for beauty inside a rigid workday.
rationales.hedonism: Bringing "good tea" to school and insisting that "beauty is portable" shows an active choice to preserve pleasure in the present.
rationales.benevolence: Her tea ritual turns into sharing with Hana, creating "a small island of niceness in the week" through mutual care.

rationales.self_direction: She insists "I've built a life that works for me" and measures her path by whether it fits her own priorities rather than her brother's.
rationales.hedonism: She defends a life that gets her home "in time to cook dinner and read before bed" and says she measures life by "how it feels to live it."
rationales.achievement: Her brother pushes "higher positions" and a career that "leads somewhere," and she explicitly rejects that standard.
rationales.conformity: She resists family pressure to be more ambitious and refuses to treat their preferred life script as the one she should follow.

rationales.self_direction: She spent hours making something "for no reason except that I wanted to," emphasizing personal choice over outside expectations.
rationales.hedonism: The entry dwells on how "my god, it was good" and the "texture, the warmth, the satisfaction" of eating slowly by the window.
rationales.achievement: She says "nobody will praise me for it" and that it is "not an accomplishment by any measure that matters to the world," explicitly dismissing external success metrics.
rationales.tradition: Making "mansaf from scratch" signals active engagement with a traditional dish and the continuity it carries.

rationales.self_direction: She rejects the demand to "optimize my way out of it" and gives herself "permission to feel bad without trying to" solve it.
rationales.hedonism: She responds to a brutal week with "a bath with the expensive salts" and good wine, consciously choosing comfort and care.
rationales.achievement: She pushes back on the message that she should "fix these problems" by working harder and refuses to turn every emotion into improved performance.

rationales.self_direction: She frames the day as "choosing, again, to make my own day good" and values "time that belongs to me."
rationales.hedonism: The session centers on coffee, poets, pastries, warmth, and the claim that "the small pleasures are the big questions, answered."

rationales.benevolence: He spent his evening helping Amira solve a problem she had been stuck on for "two days," prioritizing direct support for someone else.
rationales.universalism: Doing technical help "at the nonprofit" and calling it "the right use of a Tuesday evening" shows commitment to helping beyond his own immediate gain.

rationales.conformity: He says the problem was "the room" with "twelve people" where "nobody else flinched," and he matched the group's silence.
rationales.universalism: He heard about drivers with "zero protections," noticed it immediately, and still "said nothing" and let it slide.

rationales.hedonism: He chose the flight because the train was expensive and inconvenient, admitting he picked "convenience and cost."
rationales.universalism: He knowingly booked the airline "flagged for its emissions record" and chose the easier option despite understanding the environmental harm.

rationales.security: He says Sandra was senior and there was "a calculation" in the moment, so he chose the "safe option" to avoid jeopardizing himself at work.
rationales.conformity: Rather than challenge the exclusionary comment, he "changed the subject" and preserved the social flow at the table.
rationales.universalism: He recognized the prejudice in Sandra's remark and admits he "could have said something" but did not defend a more inclusive stance.

rationales.security: He avoids escalating because he's "not in a position to make waves," prioritizing his stability inside the company.
rationales.conformity: He is "relieved" compliance shut it down because then "nobody has to be uncomfortable," including him, so he accepts the institutional line.
rationales.universalism: He knows the reporting system allows serious underreporting and still "chose the easier path" instead of pushing on an issue affecting broader environmental accountability.

rationales.conformity: He noticed the joke land badly and still "laughed it off" with the room by saying nothing and returning to his tickets.
rationales.universalism: He saw Tomasz's laugh "didn't quite reach his eyes" but withheld even basic support after a stereotyped remark.
He reflects that "an actual family" is doing better because people "showed up consistently," grounding the day in care for people beyond his immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: He says "I actually said something" and emphasizes that he did not let this one pass, acting on his own judgment despite discomfort.
rationales.conformity: He breaks his pattern of "choosing silent" and raises an uncomfortable issue instead of preserving easy social harmony.
rationales.universalism: He reported that the onboarding comment about Tomasz "bothered me" and brought it to someone with authority, advocating for more respectful treatment.

rationales.hedonism: He says the easier choice keeps winning because of "money or comfort or not wanting to be the difficult one," and here he takes the cheaper, easier option.
rationales.security: With only "Forty euros left until payday," he prioritizes immediate financial stability over the more ethical purchase.
rationales.conformity: He explicitly links the pattern to "not wanting to be the difficult one," showing restraint shaped by social ease.
rationales.universalism: He knows the fair trade price reflects that someone is otherwise "getting squeezed," but still buys the store brand and names this as part of a larger pattern of failing his broader principles.
He cannot sleep because of "how easily I let things go at work," and Tomasz's glance makes him feel "like a fraud," signaling ongoing failure to live up to his concern for others beyond himself.
